query: TSMC A14 process node volume production timeline specifications performance improvement N2 comparison

{"query":"TSMC A14 process node volume production timeline specifications performance improvement N2 comparison","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1966,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.tsmc.com/english/dedicatedFoundry/technology/logic/l_A14","title":"A14 Technology - Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited","content":"A14 development is progressing smoothly with yield performance ahead of schedule.
A14 production is on track for 2028.
In 2022, TSMC became the first foundry to move 3nm FinFET (N3) technology into high-volume production.
In 2018, TSMC became the first foundry to start 7nm FinFET (N7) volume production.
The 22nm Ultra-Low Power (22ULP) process technology is derived from TSMC's industry-leading 28nm technology.
TSMC led the foundry segment to start the volume production of a variety of products for multiple customers using its 40nm process technology in 2008.
The Company also introduced foundry’s first 65nm Low Power (LP) process to meet..."},{"url":"https://semiwiki.com/wikis/industry-wikis/tsmc-a14-process-technology-wiki/","title":"TSMC A14 Process Technology Wiki - SemiWiki","content":"Jul 14, 2025 — Compared to TSMC N2, the A14 node delivers: +10–15% performance gain at the same power – 25–30% power reduction at the same speed +20–23% logic ...
Also known as: TSMC 1.4nm, A14 process node
Node class: Advanced logic technology (1.4nm)
Predecessor: TSMC N2 (2nm)
These improvements reflect a full node advancement, unlike the intra-node optimizations (e.g., N3E → N3P) seen in previous generations.
A14 will support 3DIC and chiplet architectures via CoWoS, InFO, and SoIC.
2028 — A14 enters high-volume manufacturing (HVM)
A14 will be a key enabler for heterogeneous integration, allowing multiple dies (different nodes and functions) to interconnect with advanced 2.5D/3D packaging."},{"url":"https://pr.tsmc.com/english/news/3228","title":"TSMC Unveils Next-Generation A14 Process at North America Technology Symposium","content":"23, 2025 - TSMC (TWSE: 2330, NYSE: TSM) today unveiled its next cutting-edge logic process technology, A14, at the Company’s North America Technology Symposium.
Representing a significant advancement from TSMC’s industry-leading N2 process, A14 is designed to drive AI transformation forward by delivering faster computing and greater power efficiency.
Volume production is scheduled for 2027.
These include silicon photonics integration with TSMC’s Compact Universal Photonic Engine (COUPE™), N12 and N3 logic base die for HBM4, and a new Integrated Voltage Regulator (IVR) for AI with 5X vertical power density delivery compared with a separate power management chip on the circuit board.
It is scheduled to enter risk production in first quarter of 2026."},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/marcomezger_tsmc-foundries-technologies-activity-7403646482878349312-A9i-","title":"TSMC claims 4.2X efficiency gain over a decade from N7 to A14 fabrication process 💡 Slowly but surely 😉 | Marco Mezger","content":"In December 2025, the "final exam" for the 2nm process officially entered the grading stage.
N2 (2nm) remains firmly on track Risk production in 2025, volume in 2026 — supported by nanosheet technology and a mature ecosystem.
A16 (1.6nm-class) officially introduced A16 adopts Backside Power Delivery (BSPDN), enabling higher energy efficiency and improved routing density.
TSMC begins 2nm chip production with 80% yields as Samsung trails Essential Information: 1."},{"url":"https://mlq.ai/earnings/highlight/TSM-tsmcs-a14-technology-to-provide-signifi-a3eb5c/","title":"TSMC's A14 technology to provide significant efficiency and performance gains | TSM Q1 2026 | MLQ.ai","content":"TSMC's A14 technology to provide significant efficiency and performance gains
With production scheduled for 2028, this technology will further extend our leadership position in high-performance and energy-efficient computing.
Compared with N2, A14 will provide 10 to 15 speed improvement at the same power or 25 to 30 power improvement at the same speed and close to 20% chip density gain."}]}

[](https://www.tsmc.com/english/dedicatedFoundry/technology/platform). ### [Technology Platforms](https://www.tsmc.com/english/dedicatedFoundry/technology/platform). *   [Overview](https://www.tsmc.com/english/dedicatedFoundry). *   [Technology](https://www.tsmc.com/english/dedicatedFoundry/technology). *   [Manufacturing](https://www.tsmc.com/english/dedicatedFoundry/manufacturing).","score":0.68140936,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://pr.tsmc.com/english/news/3228","title":"TSMC Unveils Next-Generation A14 Process at North America Technology Symposium","content":"## Showcasing TSMC’s latest offerings for high performance computing, smartphone, automotive, and IoT applications. **SANTA CLARA, CA, Apr. 23, 2025 -** TSMC (TWSE: 2330, NYSE: TSM) today unveiled its next cutting-edge logic process technology, A14, at the Company’s North America Technology Symposium. Compared with the N2 process, which is about to enter volume production later this year, A14 will offer up to 15% speed improvement at the same power, or up to 30% power reduction at the same speed, along with more than 20% increase in logic density. “TSMC’s cutting-edge logic technologies like A14 are part of a comprehensive suite of solutions that connect the physical and digital worlds to unleash our customers’ innovation for advancing the AI future.”. With TSMC’s previously announced ultra-low power N6e process now in production, the company is targeting **N4e** to continue pushing the envelope of power efficiency for future edge AIs. TSMC’s North America Technology Symposium in Santa Clara, California, is the Company’s flagship customer event of the year, with more than 2,500 people registered to attend.","score":0.6260562,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.tomshardware.com/tech-industry/semiconductors/tsmc-confirms-significant-yield-and-performance-improvements-in-a14-update-strong-interest-from-ai-hpc-and-smartphone-customers","title":"TSMC confirms significant yield and performance ...","content":"Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. TSMC's A14 (1.4nm-class) fabrication process has made rapid progress in the last three months and is well ahead of N2 at the same stage of development, according to the company's update provided at its earnings call this week. For comparison, TSMC's N2 demonstrated more than 80% of its target device performance and over 50% yield on a 256Mb SRAM test chip in April 2023. By April 2024, the process had advanced to more than 90% of its target device performance and over 80% SRAM yield. Nonetheless, the close to 90% device performance and close to 90% 256Mb SRAM yield about 2.5 years away from expected mass production start put TSMC's A14 progress well ahead of N2. *Follow* *Tom's Hardware on Google News**, or* *add us as a preferred source**, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.*.","score":0.595987,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://semiwiki.com/wikis/industry-wikis/tsmc-a14-process-technology-wiki","title":"TSMC A14 Process Technology Wiki","content":"Jul 14, 2025 — Compared to TSMC N2, the A14 node delivers: +10–15% performance gain at the same power – 25–30% power reduction at the same speed +20–23% logic","score":0.4882108,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://mosgyan.com/tsmc-2nm-production-update-a14-node-development","title":"TSMC 2nm: When in Production & A14 Node Development? - Mosgyan","content":"# TSMC 2nm: When in Production & A14 Node Development? TSMC 2nm is the latest tech node which is supposed to get into the mass production by the 2nd half of 2025. Earlier TSMC announced in the technology symposium that their 2nm tech node will be able to support backside power delivery  which is not supported in the N2P chips as it will continue to use regular  power delivery circuit. However as per some reports iPhone 17 is supposed to use TSMC’s 3nm (N3P) tech node whereas TSMC 2nm chips can be seen in the iPhone 18 series. One of the reason behind this claim is that 2nm process mass production is schedule to take place by the end of 2025 & before that apple will reveal its iphone 17 series but there are few reports which also indicates that TSMC is accelerating the production process so that stable yield can be secured for mass production.","score":0.37827587,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.24,"request_id":"be5206a1-205d-47e0-bf95-3f32616f888b"}
